title: Implement a Simple Payment Gateway
statement:
Create a payment gateway that accepts payments with the following functionality. Your server should have the following endpoints:

*   `/api/payments`: POST - to create a new payment.
*   `/api/payments/:id`: GET - to retrieve a payment by ID.

You should accept the payment details in the request body as JSON, with the following structure:

```json
{
  "amount": number,
  "currency": string
}
```

Your server should store the payment details in an in-memory array.

When creating a new payment, it should return the payment details in the response body.

When retrieving a payment by ID, it should return the corresponding payment details in the response body. If the payment is not found, it should return a 404 status code.

You should not implement any validation for the payment details, except to check that the amount is a positive number and the currency is a string.

You can assume that the payment IDs will be positive integers, and that the IDs will be unique for each payment.

You can use the `express` framework to create the server, and the `express.json()` middleware to parse the request body as JSON.

You can use the built-in `Array` data type to store the payments in memory.

You can assume that the server will be started with the environment variable `PORT` set to the port number it should listen on.
constraints: Must use only the in-memory array provided
Must implement the endpoints as described above

endpoints.description: Create a new payment. Accepts a JSON body with the payment details.
